free_text: Neutron activation cross sections for Ti isotopes
Samples of different isotopic compositions were used
(see 'METHOD'). The samples of natural isotopic abun-
dance were prepared from a thin metal foil, the others
by pressing TiO2 powder into plexiglass containers of
diameter 16 mm. Sample thickness varied from 100 to
300 mg/cm2. The isotopic abundances are given in %
. In order to resolve (n,p) and (n,np) cross
sections leading to the same activation product,
3 to 5 samples with different isotopic abundances were
used and the contributing cross sections were deduced
using a linear least squares method.
-Self-absorption of gamma rays in the sample
The statistical errors of (n,p) and (n,np) cross
sections leading to the same product (deduced from the
same activity) are correlated, their correlation coeff-
icients are given in the following Table in %.
The common systematic errors are not included in this
uncertainty and the following sources of systematic
